entries.title: odoo-ai-skills
entries.description: The local-first verification & deploy gate for AI-written Odoo 17/18/19 changes — static indexes suggest; this verifies against the running instance. Read ground truth from the live instance first (fields, MRO/super chains, views, security, runtime flow, runtime state + exception post-mortem, reverse field impact, effective per-user/company security, module load state), build the smallest correct change, prove it with tests, and review it before merge — never guess. Layer-K discovery solves the cold-start problem — rank the live entrypoint surface (buttons/crons/automations/routes), sample the real cross-app flow as an Execution Surface Graph, measure hallucination reduction against a benchmark, and ENFORCE no-introspect-no-edit via a PreToolUse hook. Layer-I enforcement gates turn that evidence into proof: risk-based scenario test generation, dev-vs-prod environment parity, a static anti-pattern validator, enforced privacy redaction, an upgrade/migration harness, a deploy approval gate, a one-command evidence bundle (PR-comment verdict), and a bring-your-own-index claim verifier; plus a local Odoo developer-docs index (Layer J). No SaaS, no seats, no metadata leaves the box. Covers models, scaffolding, views, OWL, public web (controllers/website/portal), security, reports, data, migration, performance, deploy (incl. odoo.sh), and domain playbooks.

entries.title: wicked-garden
entries.description: Your coding agent's harness already plans and swarms; Wicked Garden is the curated toolkit for what it can't do alone. Proof, not claims — gates re-derive 'done' through the internal loom engine (fail-closed, independently attested via wicked-vault), so a self-asserted pass can't lie its way green. Relationships grep can't see — codegraph + injected bus/dispatch/capability edges power blast-radius & lineage. Plus deterministic multi-file refactor (wicked-patch), cross-session memory (wicked-brain), real multi-model second opinions (jam:council), portable expertise as on-demand skill-refs, and evidence-gated testing (wicked-testing). It reads the shape of your work to apply the right rigor — steering, not a pipeline — then gets out of the way. The compiler stamps a self-contained evidence gate into any repo that runs with no wicked-garden installed. Don't fight the harness; fill its gaps. The evidence gate requires wicked-vault (installed automatically via wicked-testing); wicked-testing, wicked-brain, wicked-understanding, and wicked-bus are opt-in layers — install what you'll use, the toolkit works without the rest.

entries.title: pipeline-orchestrator
entries.description: v8.22.0 (2026-07-07, MINOR — hook-unblock pack: 5 anti-deadlock/anti-friction fixes to the enforcement layer, from the 2026-07-07 corrective-telemetry diagnosis (27 triggers; live egg-and-chicken deadlock killed run 2026-07-04-e2e-ruler-recalibration-s2 with 0/320 diff): (T1) ALREADY-DISPATCHED exemption — pendingAlreadyDispatched + dispatchTargetMatches in edit-guard-hook.cjs treat a DISPATCH_REQUEST as satisfied once the target subagent was genuinely spawned (pending_dispatches record from dispatch-record-hook, created_at >= emitted_at, leaf-containment match mirroring isResolutionAgent) — frees all 3 session blockers at once; block between REQUEST and spawn, tamper fail-closed and malformed-shape guard preserved. (T2) ANCHORED exec-window control-plane exemption in dispatch-pending-gate.cjs — a Bash/PowerShell command that IS exactly node …/scripts/exec-window/open.cjs|close.cjs (env-var prefixes tolerated) passes with a live pending; composition (&&, ;, |, backtick, newline, subshell) or the path embedded as another program argument never does (anti-injection pinned by tests). (T3) DETERMINISTIC pending clear — step-ledger-stamp.cjs (PostToolUse:Agent) clearSatisfiedPending removes the returning agent-matching DISPATCH_REQUEST entry from the signed state (withLock + read-verify-refuse-launder, record-step posture), replacing the honor-only controller prose whose omission caused a false 482-min PROTOCOL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT. (T4) DISK-TRUTH anti-false-timeout — pendingResolvedByEvents: a pending whose matching DISPATCH_RESULT is already recorded in the run protocol-events.jsonl (timestamp >= request) is RESOLVED, wired into findLivePendingBlock AND the cleanup-orphan checkHandshakeTimeouts sweep (implementing its own docstring promise "AND no matching response arrived"); DISPATCH-only scope (GATE/PLAN_MODE stay human-resolved). (T5) STAMP-BY-EVIDENCE — step-ledger-gate.cjs grants the tdd step when gate-decisions.jsonl carries TDD_APPROVAL APPROVED|CONFIRMED (frozen RED tests from a prior session), persists the stamp via recordStep and leaves a STEP_STAMP_BY_EVIDENCE audit event in protocol-events (NOT a new gate — Iron Law preserved: Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED). Tests tests/regression/v8.22.0/F1-F5 (41 scenarios, TDD RED->GREEN per task); suite 258/8 = pre-existing baseline exactly (3x Langfuse + score-writer + F4 v8.8.0 + 3x v8.21.0 frozen RED from the in-flight S2 slice), 0 new failures. Authored via the loopify harness (loop/hook-unblock branch, per-task selective commits); headless loop hit the harness sensitive-file wall on .claude/hooks/** — implementation landed from the interactive session, lesson recorded in loops/hook-unblock/LOOP_DONE.md. Anterior: v8.20.0 (2026-07-02, MINOR — enforcement unblocking pack from the 2026-07-02 hook audit): (1) STOP-TRAP FAIL-OPEN: the three Stop-family gates (stop-gate-hook, e2e-eval-gate, subagent-stop-commit-hook) blocked FOREVER when their continuity counter could not be persisted (signer unavailable / verifyState failing at write time / read-only state or dir) — persist()/withSignedState() now report real commit success and each block site fails OPEN with an audit trace, extending the existing !statePath B5 guard to every unpersistable shape. (2) scope-lock-hook emits the canonical hookSpecificOutput PreToolUse envelope — the old FLAT top-level shape was not honored by the harness, so the refactor scope deny was either ignored or bound without its reason ever reaching the model. (3) PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED / CORRUPT_STATE denies + the arm-writer/session-arm banners rewritten to ONE reachable next action (dispatch Agent(subagent_type: pipeline-orchestrator:core:pipeline-controller)) — the impossible write-sentinel-state-by-hand recipe (which contradicted MARKER_TAMPERED and deepened the block) and the literal {PIPELINE_DOC_PATH}/<cwd> placeholders are GONE. (4) lib/pipeline-arm.cjs HARNESS_OPEN_TAG now also cuts teammate-message/agent-message envelopes — a background subagent report QUOTING /pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline continue in prose armed a phantom CONTINUE run live on 2026-07-02, bricking the session Bash/PowerShell/Agent until the TTL. (5) NEW .claude/hooks/sentinel-state-autosign.cjs (PostToolUse Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit): signs the Bash-less controller freshly-written UNSIGNED run-dir sentinel-state.json (NEVER touches a present signature — invalid = tamper evidence; SKIPS under PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT; fail-open) — closes STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL and most corrective-loop noise; zero security delta (unsigned was already tolerated by default). TDD tests/regression/v8.20.0/F1-F5 (26 scenarios RED-to-GREEN incl. a live-repro of the phantom arm). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/ + lib/ + hooks/hooks.json + tests/; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 257/5 (same 5 pre-existing). Lockstep all version surfaces. v8.19.3 (2026-07-01, PATCH — arm-classifier: keyword-inferred read-only runs no longer arm [recurring phantom-arm deadlock fix]): a natural-language review/audit/analysis request ('faça revisão adversarial', 'faça uma auditoria', 'análise') was classified FULL/Audit and armed the pipeline-arm-pending marker — but read-only work never opens an exec window, so it hit INLINE_WORK_BLOCKED, aborted, and left an orphan marker that bricked the next task with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED (observed live in interview-coach 2026-06-30 and 07-01). FIX: lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::writeArmPending now also skips arming for the read-only TYPES (Audit, UX Simulation) when they were INFERRED FROM KEYWORDS (source kw:), while an EXPLICIT --audit/--ux flag stays a deliberate governed run and still arms (preserving the STEP_1_7 direct-entry evidence, v8.16.0). Safe direction per the documented arm trade-off: a missed arm is recoverable (re-invoke); a phantom arm deadlocks. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.3/F1-readonly-types-no-arm (RED 4-fail → GREEN); 2 deliberate v8.7.0 pins updated to the --audit-flag path. Iron Law: only lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Gates/Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 252/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep across all version surfaces. v8.19.2 (2026-07-01, PATCH — edit-guard-hook robustness [Cursor-crash hardening, Claude-Code-scoped]): the PreToolUse edit-guard no longer crashes into the opaque external catch ('edit-guard-hook internal error — failing closed') when .pipeline/sessions is present-but-unreadable (a file where a dir is expected → ENOTDIR/EACCES) — getActiveLock + getActiveExecWindow now guard readdirSync and return null instead of throwing, and the CLI external catch surfaces the REAL sanitized error (single-line, printable-ASCII, capped 200) instead of the opaque message, so a genuine failure stays fail-closed but debuggable. Posture UNCHANGED (still deny when the guard cannot evaluate); NO foreign tool-name vocabulary added — cross-runtime tool mapping is the other editor's concern, deliberately out of scope. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.2/F1 (RED 7-fail → GREEN, 8 checks). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/edit-guard-hook.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 251/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.19.1 (2026-06-30, PATCH — arm-marker consume-on-close deadlock fix): the pipeline-arm-pending marker (written at /pipeline invocation, 30-min TTL) was never consumed when a governed run ended deliberately, so a finished code-mutating run's still-live marker kept blocking the NEXT non-pipeline task in the same session with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED until TTL/restart (REVIEW-ONLY/DIAGNOSTIC never arm — the trigger is a FULL/code run that armed, completed, and was never disarmed). FIX: stop-gate-hook.cjs consumes the marker on its closed-run ALLOW paths (completed/GO-completer/spec-seal/hard_failed-cap/deliberate-inactive) via a new session-aware lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::clearArmPendingForSession — never on a block, on SessionEnd/StopFailure recovery, or on corrupt state. Positive-ownership delete (deletes only when marker+caller session_ids are both present and equal, OR both absent; ambiguous/mismatched preserved for TTL) + tamper verdict delegated to the lib/arm-pending SSOT (a signed-but-invalid marker stays fail-closed). 2 zero-context adversarial rounds (1 HIGH tamper-bypass + 1 CRITICAL cross-session-delete, both fixed) then a final pass CLEAN. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.1/F1 (10 scenarios, all 5 call sites + tamper + ownership). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/stop-gate-hook.cjs + lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 250/5 (5 pre-existing). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.17.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix run delivers 4 audit-driven fixes + 1 meta-escape) — (AUDIT-001 MED-LOW) `.claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs` adds tail-read verify after `appendRunLog`: on mismatch, a single `run-log verify mismatch` warn fires on stderr; soft-fail on missing/malformed; exported as `verifyRunLogAppend` for unit testing. (AUDIT-004 CRIT, deployment) new `lib/enforcement-status.cjs::detectEnforcementStatus({hooksDir, expectedCanonicalHook})` probes the install for the canonical execution-gate hook (`pipeline-arm-gate.cjs`); wired into the existing SessionStart `enforcement-surface-verify-hook.cjs` to emit a dedicated `ENFORCEMENT_INACTIVE` protocol-event with HIGH severity when missing — registered in `lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs::HYGIENE_EVENT_SEVERITY` at weight 1.0 so an install with the gate missing cannot reach clean ProcessHygiene. (AUDIT-005 HIGH) `lib/audit-read-budget.cjs::buildAuditCorrectiveDescriptor` adds closed-vocabulary machine-readable fields `corrective_action:"dispatch_agent"` + `agent_type:"audit-intake"`; `buildAuditInlineReason` appends a `[corrective_action=dispatch_agent agent_type=audit-intake]` suffix to the prose reason so a parent reading only text can still pattern-match the recovery action. (AUDIT-006 NEW MED) `lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs::scoreRun` short-circuits on aborted runs (`state.abort_reason` non-empty + `pipeline_outcome` starting with `ABORTED_`): drops StepCompletion / GateCoverage / OrderIntegrity (renormalized over ProcessHygiene), emits exactly one AUDIT-class `hygiene_violation` named `PIPELINE_ABORTED` severity=high weight=1.0 — so aborted runs surface as aborts instead of being scored as catastrophic failures. (META — user-authorized chain-tied deadlock escape) `.claude/hooks/skill-frontmatter-parser.cjs::getEnforcementMode` honours the `PIPELINE_AUTONOMOUS_RECOVERY_MODE=true` env flag by DOWNGRADING `deny` to `warn` for the process (never escalates `warn`; case-sensitive `"true"` only; one stderr audit line per process). The flag institutionalizes the user-authorized self-recovery path: when a parent must dispatch multiple agents in sequence under autonomous /goal full autonomy, the chain-tied enforcement converts to WARN so the run can complete instead of deadlocking at the gate. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.17.0/F1-F5 (5 files, 35+ cases); full suite 4 fail (the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env baseline — F8/F9/F10 langfuse + F2 score-writer — unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/`, `.codex/hooks/`, `lib/` and `tests/` touched; the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.16.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix of 2 glitches caught by the v8.15.0 E2E evaluator during the v8.15.1 self-audit run, both deterministic-evaluator findings): (Glitch A — HIGH, missing_step:final) the step-ledger-stamp.cjs PostToolUse:Agent path was already correct on the standard Agent return shape — F1 in tests/regression/v8.16.0/ now PINS that contract (final-validator return → `final` stamped; raw V1 fence string accepted; errored/ungoverned returns rejected), so a future regression to the stamping path is caught immediately. entries.title: email-monitor
entries.description: Use when you want unattended Gmail triage on this machine: a heartbeat tick incrementally watches one or more inboxes over read-only IMAP (UID + UIDVALIDITY watermark, BODY.PEEK, X-GM-MSGID dedupe), classifies each new message by acted-on importance via a three-tier gate (L0 rules / L1 cheap scoring / L2 LLM hook), pushes a redacted ASCII-only Discord alert for URGENT/ACTION mail, archives NOISE through the existing label tool, tracks the resulting affairs in the shared schedule-reminder task pool with deadline normalization (NY to UTC, DST-correct), drafts concise compliance-checked replies as review-only Gmail drafts (never auto-sent), and emits a daily digest. Reuses the on-machine IMAP toolchain, reminder base, and Discord relay rather than rebuilding any store, scheduler, or notifier; app passwords stay in DPAPI and mail bodies never leave the local session. Route bulk relabeling to the raw gmail-imap-label tool, generic non-mail reminders to schedule-reminder, and actual sending to the user by hand.
